intro: Neomammillaria subangularis, also known as the "Mexican snowball cactus," is a small, globular cactus species native to Mexico, characterized by its vibrant flowers and unique, snowball-like growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria subangularis include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
